name: Home overview dashboard unification across spending, budgets, and household activity
description: Rebuild the current lightweight home/overview page into the real top-level dashboard of the application. This is the broadest and highest-impact gap-closure feature because it needs to unify information that already exists across expenses, budgets, analytics, onboarding, and household management into one coherent first screen. The target outcome is a dashboard that helps users understand current financial state, take the next obvious action quickly, and navigate household context without hunting across separate pages. This work should stay intentionally integrative: reuse existing domain surfaces where possible, but raise the quality of the app's primary entry experience to match the maturity of the underlying feature set.
evidence: Completed frontend-first `/home` dashboard unification on 2026-05-08, then completed a primitive-first home hardening follow-up on 2026-05-12. The latest pass migrated `apps/web/src/views/app/overview-page.tsx` and the touched `apps/web/src/components/home/*` sections back onto shared `components/ui` primitives, added pill toggle-group support for lenses, a reusable `Badge` filter variant, chart tones on `Progress`, glass-card composition for loading and content states, and finance-safe `font-mono tabular-nums` amount styling, while localizing touched overview copy in `apps/web/src/lib/i18n/locales/vi.json`. Focused verification kept only primitive behavior tests per user instruction not to add component render tests: `pnpm --filter web test -- --run src/components/ui/toggle-group.test.tsx src/components/ui/progress.test.tsx`, `pnpm --filter web test`, `pnpm lint:fix`, `./init.sh`, and `gitnexus_detect_changes` (scope `all`) → low risk, 0 affected processes. Backend summary support remained unnecessary.
